findings.evidence: `rg` hits: Mathlib has `Submodule.eq_top_iff'` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Submodule/Lattice.lean:163` and `Algebra.eq_top_iff` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Algebra/Subalgebra/Lattice.lean:247`; the PR reproves the same shape at lines 96 and 147.
findings.fix: Remove these declarations and use `Submodule.eq_top_iff'` and `Algebra.eq_top_iff` at call sites, or keep only a very thin alias if there is a demonstrated downstream need.
findings.issue: `matrixCoefficientSubmodule_eq_top_iff` and `matrixCoefficientSubalgebra_eq_top_iff` are special cases of existing generic top-membership lemmas.

findings.evidence: Mathlib exposes the generic ingredients at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Algebra/Subalgebra/Lattice.lean:715` (`span_le_adjoin`) and `:82` (`toSubmodule_eq_top`); the PR instead runs `Submodule.span_induction` over `matrixCoefficientSet` at lines 165-173.
findings.fix: Prove a general lemma, e.g. `Algebra.adjoin_eq_top_of_span_eq_top {s : Set A} (h : Submodule.span R s = ⊤) : Algebra.adjoin R s = ⊤`, using `Algebra.span_le_adjoin` and `Algebra.toSubmodule_eq_top`, then derive the matrix-coefficient corollary from it.
findings.issue: `matrixCoefficientSubalgebra_eq_top_of_submodule_eq_top` hand-proves the generic fact that if `Submodule.span R s = ⊤`, then `Algebra.adjoin R s = ⊤`, specialized to `matrixCoefficientSet`.

summary: The new coefficient-generated objects are at the right domain level, but several lemmas package generic lattice/algebra facts under matrix-coefficient names. Generalize those facts or use the existing Mathlib API instead of adding special-case API.
